title: Cyrtochilum hastiferum
taxonomy: Cyrtochilum hastiferum is classified within the Orchidaceae family, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. It belongs to the subfamily Cypripedioideae. Cyrtochilum hastiferum is also classified as a genus within the subtribe Zygopelmatinae. Further classification places it within the tribe Cypripedieae. Cyrtochilum hastiferum's classification also includes its placement in the family Orchidaceae, which is further divided into several subfamilies.
uses: Cyrtochilum hastiferum is a species of orchid, and like many orchids, it has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. However, specific information on its medicinal uses is not readily available. Cyrtochilum hastiferum may be used in traditional medicine in some regions, but its effectiveness and safety for human consumption or topical application have not been extensively scientifically verified. More research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and risks associated with using Cyrtochilum hastiferum for medicinal purposes.
